entry.title: Combined summary - The Four Horsemen
entry.summary: The unified specification "The Four Horsemen (of the Blockchain)" presents a comprehensive approach to enhancing Bitcoin's on-chain privacy through the integration of multiple existing technologies into a single system. This initiative aims to address issues such as fragmentation and liquidity problems in current coinjoin protocols, while simultaneously minimizing fees and optimizing block space usage. The specification comprises several subprotocols, including WabiSabi, ZeroLink, JoinMarket, and PayJoin. Each of these subprotocols brings unique advantages: WabiSabi allows for UTXO consolidation without ownership disclosure, ZeroLink offers efficient remixing with DoS protection, JoinMarket provides on-demand liquidity, particularly benefiting high-volume traders, and PayJoin enhances third-party privacy during direct transactions, supported by implementations accessible at [Payjoin v1](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0078.mediawiki) and [Payjoin v2](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0077.md). These subprotocols are designed to mitigate specific weaknesses and costs associated with Bitcoin transactions, such as slow transaction times, unspendable change amounts, trust dependencies, and potential points of failure in privacy. Moreover, the specification delves into incentive structures and user experience (UX) elements, showing how different technologies can be combined to cater to varying needs like cost efficiency, liquidity, or privacy maximization. The protocol design not only anticipates future enhancements but also emphasizes the necessity of wallet synchronization through compact block filters or full nodes by default, which ensures a robust implementation focused on user requirements. The document remains open for community feedback, underlining the importance of continuous development and refinement to achieve optimal privacy and utility in Bitcoin transactions. Recent updates to "The Four Horsemen" project highlight significant improvements in intermediary integrations and system performance. The development team has detailed a fully defined UX path that prioritizes intuitive navigation and engagement, ensuring a seamless interaction for end-users. These advancements demonstrate a proactive approach in leveraging new technologies to enhance functionality, scalability, and reliability, preparing the system for broader market applications. This strategic deployment positions the project well within the competitive landscape and underscores a strong commitment to quality and user-centric design, laying a solid foundation for future developments and potential market growth.
